fundamentals.name: CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc. provides cybersecurity solutions in the United States and internationally. Its unified platform provides cloud-delivered protection of endpoints, cloud workloads, identity, and data through a software as a service (SaaS) subscription-based model. The company offers corporate endpoint and cloud workload security, managed security, security and vulnerability management, IT operations management, identity protection, threat intelligence, data protection, SaaS security posture management, and AI powered workflow automation, and securing generative AI workload services, as well as security orchestration, automation, and response; and security information and event management, and log management services. It primarily sells subscriptions to its Falcon platform and cloud modules. The company was incorporated in 2011 and is headquartered in Austin, Texas.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue and free cash flow, but currently unprofitable on a net basis and trading at a premium valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 1.6%; next earnings date is 2026-06-03.
positives: Revenue growth 23.3% YoY, with analyst estimates for current Q and FY showing strong expected growth.
Free cash flow (TTM) of $1.60B indicates healthy operational cash generation.
Gross margin of 74.8% is robust, suggesting strong pricing power.
Total cash of $5.23B provides ample liquidity, with debt levels appearing manageable.
negatives: Profit margin is negative at -3.4%, and return on equity/assets are also negative, indicating the company is not yet profitable.
Forward P/E of 108.91 and Price/Sales of 35.48 suggest a very high valuation.
Mean analyst target of $527.63 is below the current spot price, indicating potential overvaluation according to some analysts.

news.paragraph: CrowdStrike (CRWD) is seeing significant positive attention, with the stock earning a spot back on an elite list and cyber stocks, including CRWD, Cisco, and Palo Alto, hitting records. Morgan Stanley recently made a substantial adjustment to its price target for CrowdStrike, while the company also announced new visibility and monitoring capabilities for Claude via its Compliance API. These developments suggest strong momentum and ongoing innovation in the cybersecurity space.
